comment: Our daughter started piano at seven and the teacher makes theory fun with little games. She actually practices without us nagging.
replyText: Thank you, Priya — we love hearing when practice feels like play at home too!

comment: Guitar group class on Saturdays is well organised. Clear handouts and they split beginners from kids who already read tabs.
Vocal lessons helped my son’s confidence for the school musical. Only wish there were a few more evening slots.
replyText: Thanks, Helen — we’ve just opened one more Tuesday 6:30 slot; happy to move you if it helps.

comment: I’m an adult learner on violin. Zero judgement, pace matched to my aching wrists, and they loaned a shoulder rest the first month.
Drum tuition is energetic and my teenager finally cares about timing. Studio can get loud in the waiting area — bring headphones.
Trinity exam prep was structured week by week. Mock exam feedback was honest and kind — she passed with merit.
Lessons are good but the invoice email landed in spam twice. Tuition is fair; admin comms could be clearer.
replyText: Sorry about that, Elena — we’ve switched to a clearer subject line and SMS reminders for invoices.

comment: Ukulele summer crash course was worth it. Small class, everyone left strumming three songs confidently.
Keyboard lab has decent headphones. Teacher records short clips of tricky sections so we can replay at home — huge help.
My twins share a back-to-back slot. Front desk is friendly and there’s a little table for homework between lessons.
We had to cancel three weeks for travel. Make-up policy was stricter than we expected for the price of private tuition.
replyText: We’re sorry that was frustrating. Our policy is on the enrolment form — we’ll call you to see if we can offer a one-off courtesy credit.

comment: Cello instructor explains bow weight in plain language. My posture headaches stopped after two lessons of adjustments.
Rock band workshop for tweens — loud but supervised. Kids wrote a simple chorus and performed it on the last day.
Music theory used to bore my son; now they tie every scale to a song he likes. Grade 5 feels achievable.
Trial lesson was great but we waited two weeks for a regular flute slot. Worth it once we got in.
Recorder club for Year 2 — adorable concert at term end. Teachers clearly care about stage nerves.
Bass guitar lessons connect songs to ear training. Parking is tight on weekday evenings; arrive ten minutes early.
Hybrid option saved us during flu season — same teacher, same pieces, camera angle shows hands clearly.
Saxophone hire bundle with reeds was convenient. Explained how humidity affects the reed so we stopped wasting money.
Hindustani vocal basics — patient with my pronunciation and sends short warm-up audios between classes.
We were double-booked once and the replacement teacher didn’t know our son’s pieces. It was sorted the next week but left a bad taste.
replyText: We’re truly sorry — that should not happen. We’ve tightened our cover-lesson briefing checklist and would like to offer a complimentary lesson.

comment: Djembe circle for beginners — infectious energy. Fees transparent; no hidden “performance package” upsell.
Clarinet embouchure fixes in one session what YouTube couldn’t in a month. Waiting room could use one more chair.
Music production intro for teens — they learned a simple beat in GarageBand and the ethics of sampling. Very current.
Brass teacher is strict about daily buzzing exercises but progress on high notes was obvious within a month.
Sibling discount on back-to-back piano and violin made continuing both affordable this year.
Good teaching but the metronome app they recommend has ads; would prefer one bundled recommendation without ads for kids.
replyText: Thanks, Steve — we’ve added a free, ad-free metronome to our student portal resources page.

comment: Autism-friendly approach: breaks, visual schedule, and no pressure to perform until our child felt ready. Grateful.
Choir harmony sectionals are well run. Sheet music scans could be higher resolution on the portal — minor nitpick.
Overall the best music tuition we’ve tried in the area — professional, warm, and students actually sound better term on term.
